summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple guided bomb strikes by Russian tactical aviation across Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and other regions, alongside numerous UAV incursions targeting cities in the north and east. Russian air activity alerts indicate heightened threat of strikes on southeastern and southern fronts. Ukrainian air defenses continue to engage UAVs over several oblasts.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and eastern Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
UAVs were observed moving from Sumy toward Nizhyn district in Chernihiv, from north toward Poltava, and over Pavlohrad heading north (Source: @kpszsu/64894, @kpszsu/64887, @kpszsu/64888).
Multiple UAVs entered Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Odesa and Synelnykivka districts with various headings including northwest, south and west (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Russian tactical aviation activity alerts were issued for southeastern, eastern and southern directions, warning of possible air strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64908, @kpszsu/64922).
UAVs passed over Kharkiv near Zolochiv and Bohodukhiv heading south (Source: @kpszsu/64909).
Guided bomb strikes were also reported in eastern Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
UAVs were sighted heading toward Solone in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/64919).
UAV activity continued in the north with a reactive UAV approaching Poltava from the north (Source: @kpszsu/64887).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple guided bomb strikes by Russian tactical aviation across Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and other regions, alongside numerous UAV incursions targeting cities in the north and east. Russian air activity alerts indicate heightened threat of strikes on southeastern and southern fronts. Ukrainian air defenses continue to engage UAVs over several oblasts.

points: Guided aviation bombs were dropped by Russian tactical aviation on the Zaporizhzhia region, causing operational disruption (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
A UAV launched from Sumy was observed heading toward the Nizhyn district of Chernihiv, indicating a north‑west penetration route (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
UAV activity was reported over Poltava from the north, suggesting a direct approach toward central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64887).
In Dnipropetrovsk, a UAV passed near Pavlohrad heading north, and another UAV moved toward Solone, highlighting continued pressure on the region (Source: @kpszsu/64888, @kpszsu/64919).
UAVs were sighted in the Synelnykivka district of Dnipropetrovsk moving northwest, and in the west of Donetsk heading toward Novomykolaivka (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb launches on the eastern sector of Kharkiv, demonstrating continued offensive capability (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
Additional guided bomb strikes were reported on Kharkiv overall, reinforcing pressure on Ukrainian defenses (Source: @kpszsu/64874).
Russian air units issued alerts of increased activity on southeastern and southern axes, indicating preparation for further strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64908, @kpszsu/64922).
UAVs were observed operating from Russian‑controlled areas toward Ukrainian territory, including routes through Odesa heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Guided bomb attacks targeted eastern Kharkiv, reflecting intensified Russian focus on this front (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
UAVs entered Donetsk westward toward Novomykolaivka, suggesting attempts to disrupt logistics in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
A UAV from Sumy moved toward Nizhyn district, crossing the northeastern approach to the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
UAV activity over Dnipropetrovsk, including a northbound flight near Pavlohrad, indicates pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/64888).
A reactive UAV approached Poltava from the north, highlighting threats to central‑northern command centers (Source: @kpszsu/64887).
UAV launched from Sumy toward Nizhyn district in Chernihiv demonstrates north‑west penetration (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
UAVs were observed over Synelnykivka district heading northwest, potentially targeting northern logistical hubs (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
UAVs passed near Zolochiv and Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv heading south, indicating movement from northern positions (Source: @kpszsu/64909).
Russian tactical aviation alerts warned of heightened activity on the southern direction, implying possible upcoming strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64922).
UAV incursions were reported in the southeastern direction, adding to the threat environment for southern Ukrainian forces (Source: @kpszsu/64908).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs in multiple oblasts, including Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, showing sustained air strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/64894, @kpszsu/64871).
Alerts of enemy tactical aviation activity were issued for southeastern, eastern and southern axes, indicating a broadening of air operations (Source: @kpszsu/64908, @kpszsu/64922).
UAV sightings across northern, central and eastern Ukraine demonstrate persistent low‑altitude threats to Ukrainian airspace (Source: @kpszsu/64887, @kpszsu/64897).

UAVs were observed heading toward strategic locations such as Pavlohrad, Solone and Novomykolaivka, suggesting targeted reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/64888, @kpszsu/64919, @kpszsu/64897).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation dropped guided aviation bombs on the Zaporizhzhia region, marking a direct strike on Ukrainian territory (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
Multiple UAVs entered Ukrainian airspace from Sumy, heading toward Nizhyn district in Chernihiv, indicating a north‑west penetration route (Source: @kpszsu/64894).
Guided bomb attacks were reported on eastern Kharkiv, reinforcing Russian pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
UAVs were observed over Dnipropetrovsk near Pavlohrad heading north and toward Solone, showing continued aerial threats in central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64888, @kpszsu/64919).
Russian tactical aviation activity alerts warned of heightened threat on southeastern and southern directions, suggesting preparation for further strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64908, @kpszsu/64922).
UAVs passed over Kharkiv near Zolochiv and Bohodukhiv heading south, indicating movement from northern positions toward the front lines (Source: @kpszsu/64909).
UAVs operated in the west of Donetsk heading toward Novomykolaivka, potentially targeting logistics in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations continue to pose a significant threat across multiple fronts, with guided bomb strikes in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia demonstrating capability to strike deep targets. UAV incursions from the north, east and south increase pressure on Ukrainian air defenses and complicate command‑and‑control. The alerts of heightened tactical aviation activity suggest Russia may be preparing coordinated strikes on southeastern and southern axes, potentially aiming to disrupt logistics and frontline positions.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are actively tracking and engaging UAVs across several oblasts, employing air defense assets to mitigate the low‑altitude threat. Coordination between regional command centers appears focused on protecting key logistics hubs such as Pavlohrad and Solone. The repeated alerts indicate that Ukrainian air defense readiness is being elevated, especially in the north and east where UAV activity is most frequent.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate potential strike vectors toward critical infrastructure. The pattern of guided bomb deployments suggests Russia is targeting both frontline and rear‑area assets to degrade Ukrainian operational depth. Integration of real‑time air threat data with ground force movements will be crucial for maintaining defensive resilience on the eastern and southern fronts.
